topic.what: Detect anomalous internal model mechanisms using functional attribution to trusted reference samples
topic.how: Use Bayesian influence functions via SGLD sampling to measure correlation between test sample losses and trusted set losses; low correlation indicates anomalous mechanism
topic.why_matters: Existing latent-space methods are vulnerable to obfuscation attacks; this provides a decorrelated detection signal that works across modalities and resists adversarial evasion

characteristics.what_it_measures: Defense Effectiveness Rating for backdoor detection
characteristics.evidence: Table 1: average across 7 attacks on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, GTSRB, Tiny-ImageNet
characteristics.context: 5% poisoning rate; outperforms all 6 baselines including TECO, STRIP, ABL, i-BAU, NC, DDE

characteristics.what_it_measures: k-NN AUROC for backdoor detection in UMAP space
characteristics.context: CIFAR-10 backdoored model with checkerboard trigger
Same CIFAR-10 model; OOD samples form distinct cluster from clean

characteristics.what_it_measures: k-NN AUROC for adversarial sample detection
characteristics.context: Adversarial samples detectable but with lower separation than backdoor/OOD

characteristics.what_it_measures: AUROC for backdoor detection in language models
characteristics.context: Gemma 2-2B with LoRA; includes obfuscated models where latent methods fail

characteristics.what_itasures: Performance improvement from offline to online detection
characteristics.context: Offline UMAP clustering leverages inter-sample correlations among anomalous samples

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of SGLD parameter samples required
characteristics.evidence: Implementation details mention Ndraws as hyperparameter; sensitivity in Appendix E.1
characteristics.context: Performance stable across reasonable ranges; full specs in Appendix D
